2606.13020.title: SciR: A Controllable Benchmark for Scientific Reasoning in LLMs
2606.13020.paper_summary: Three paradigmatic forms of inference recur across scientific reasoning: deduction, induction, and causal abduction. Reliably evaluating LLMs on these in scientific settings is currently out of reach: scientific benchmarks built on human annotations are costly and lack mechanistic ground truth, while synthetic logical-reasoning benchmarks do not resemble real scientific documents. We introduce SciR, a benchmark that combines multi-paradigm reasoning with controllable scientific rendering, anchored on three paradigmatic scientific problems. Tasks are generated from formal objects (deduction tree, inductive rule hypothesis, causal graph) to guarantee verifiable answers, then rendered into multi-document scientific discourse via per-track domain-tuned genres. The construction lets us independently vary two difficulty axes: how hard it is to extract the key information needed for inference, and how hard the principled inference itself is. We test six models. Both axes hurt every model, and their effects compound. The rendering even hurts neurosymbolic pipelines, which hand inference to a verified solver. The two axes yield a per-model extraction-vs-inference profile: for instance, reasoning models like deepseek-r1 mostly surpass non-reasoning instruct models on the inference axis. To our knowledge, SciR is the first multi-paradigm scientific-reasoning benchmark with parametric control on both extraction and inference difficulty.
2606.13020.paper_summary_zh: 三種範例推理形式在科學推理中反覆出現：演繹、歸納和因果推理。當前在科學環境中可靠地評估大型語言模型（LLMs）仍然無法實現：基於人類註釋的科學基準成本高昂且缺乏機械性真實性，而合成的邏輯推理基準則與真實的科學文件不相似。我們介紹了SciR，一個結合多範式推理與可控科學呈現的基準，基於三個範例科學問題。任務由正式對象（演繹樹、歸納規則假設、因果圖）生成，以保證可驗證的答案，然後通過每個領域調整的類型轉換為多文件的科學話語。這一構建使我們能夠獨立變化兩個難度軸：提取推理所需的關鍵信息有多困難，以及原則性推理本身有多困難。我們測試了六個模型。這兩個軸對每個模型都有負面影響，且其效果相互疊加。即使是將推理交給經過驗證的解算器的神經符號管道也受到影響。這兩個軸產生了每個模型的提取與推理特徵：例如，像deepseek-r1這樣的推理模型在推理軸上大多超越了非推理指令模型。據我們所知，SciR是第一個在提取和推理難度上具有參數控制的多範式科學推理基準。
2606.13020.author: Pierre Beckmann et.al.
2606.13020.authors: Pierre Beckmann, Marco Valentino, Andre Freitas
